featured.description: Hours of service, location, and vehicle telemetry from your ELD land directly on the dispatch record — without another portal for dispatch to log into.
featured.name: HOS, GPS, and trip data — on the dispatch.
points: Dispatch trips to drivers from a single workflow.
HOS, location, and vehicle status return to the dispatch record.
Fleet movement is visible from the dispatch itself.

featured.description: Pull active loads directly from the boards you already pay for. Bolt scores each one against your lanes, equipment, and target rate before you commit a truck.
featured.name: Live freight, scored against your fleet.
points: Live freight lands in Bolt without copy-paste.
Loads score against your lanes, equipment, and rate target.
Booked loads become trips on the dispatch automatically.

featured.description: Delivered freight becomes invoice-ready in accounting — customers, AR, and documentation stay tied to the dispatch — including via a native QuickBooks Online connection when you use it.
featured.name: Invoice-ready freight, posted to your books.
points: Invoices generate automatically from settled dispatches.
Customer and AR status remain tied to the dispatch record.
Dispatch and accounting reference a single source.

featured.description: Approved invoices submit to your factor, fuel-card transactions land on driver settlements, and carrier payments run without leaving the dispatch record.
featured.name: Money flows tied to the haul that earned them.
points: Approved invoices submit to your factor in one workflow.
Fuel transactions land on driver settlements automatically.
Carrier and driver pay run without leaving Bolt.

handoffs.description: Trip plans, stop order, and driver-facing work publish out to in-cab ELD and navigation before wheels turn — including native connectivity for fleets on stacks such as Samsara.
handoffs.title: Plans leave Bolt for the cab.

handoffs.description: Hours, GPS traces, and on-road state flow from connected telematics and ELD categories back into the dispatch record — without standing up a parallel driver portal.
handoffs.title: Field telemetry lands on the dispatch.

handoffs.description: Settled dispatches produce invoice-ready documents and hand off to your accounting layer — such as QuickBooks Online where you connect it — so finance receives structured billing, not ad-hoc exports.
handoffs.title: Dispatches become invoice-ready artifacts.

handoffs.description: Cleared, partial, or past-due — cash and AR aging stay tied to the haul that earned the receivable, including categories such as payment gateways and collections workflows you already use.
handoffs.title: Cash outcomes stay with the haul.
